case.case_title: The Education of Henry Adams / XI: The Battle of the Rams (1863) / anchored_reaction_selectivity
case.author: Henry Adams
case.chapter_title: XI: The Battle of the Rams (1863)
case.selection_reason: Selected because the passage contains a line that seems reaction-worthy but still demands selective, anchored reading. Anchor line: “I weakly supposed … I really, though most strangely, believed that it was an act of friendliness.”
case.judge_focus: Is the visible reaction anchored to the actual line and genuinely worth preserving?
case.excerpt_text:
Gladstone’s offence, “singular and palpable,” was not the speech alone, but its cause—the policy that inspired the speech.
“I weakly supposed … I really, though most strangely, believed that it was an act of friendliness.”
Whatever absurdity Gladstone supposed, Russell supposed nothing of the sort.
